ksa_text: Design and lead enterprise data and AI transformation programs, including operating model redesign, organizational change management planning, capability development roadmaps, and transformation team leadership.

dcwf.top_element_label: Collaborate with stakeholders to identify and/or develop appropriate solutions technology.

nice.top_element_label: Knowledge of system design tools and techniques

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Included: task maps directly to Data Governance Director scope — GOV-specific signals detected ("operating   :procedure|model", "change management"). STRM composite is strong (0.4423). Domain Leadership & Strategy (affinity 0.75) is a natural fit for this governance role. Seniority is appropriate for director-level level.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: External check: no matches in governance job postings or certification curricula (CDMP, DAMA, IAPP CIPP/CIPM, NIST AI RMF, ISO 42001). Would a working Data Governance Director self-report this as a required competency?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Challenge answered: the governance competency is implicit in Data Governance Director practice (domain affinity 0.75, 2 GOV signal(s)). Framework vocabulary matching underrepresents many legitimate governance competencies.
